STYLING RULES:
- Dark theme: bg-dark-900, border-dark-800, text-white, text-dark-400 for secondary text
- Gold accents: text-gold-500, bg-gold-600 for primary buttons
- Font classes: font-heading for titles, font-body for body text  
- Use animate-in fade-in for entry animations

COMPONENT RULES - CRITICAL:
- ONLY import from these existing UI components: Button (@/components/ui/button), Badge (@/components/ui/badge), Input (@/components/ui/input)
- ONLY import icons from lucide-react (Clock, User, CheckCircle, Calendar, etc.)
- Do NOT import Card, ScrollArea, Avatar, Tabs, or any component not listed above
- Do NOT install new npm packages
- Use standard HTML divs with Tailwind classes for layout

Phase 4b: Build real-time analytics dashboard + SMS appointment reminders for Barber CRM

The Barber CRM at /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/ needs its analytics and notification systems completed.

1. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/revenue/page.tsx — The monthly revenue trend chart uses hardcoded static data instead of real Supabase queries. Wire it to GET /api/revenue which already exists. The line chart should show actual monthly revenue from completed appointments. The pie chart should show real revenue breakdown by service type.

2. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/notifications/page.tsx — The notification history shows 3 hardcoded mock entries. Wire it to GET /api/notifications which has Supabase integration. Show real notification history with status (sent/failed), timestamps, and recipient info. Add a button to manually send an SMS reminder for upcoming appointments (call POST /api/notifications).

3. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/reviews/page.tsx — Check if reviews page fetches from GET /api/reviews or uses mock data. If mock, wire to real Supabase data. Add average rating display and rating distribution bar chart (5 stars, 4 stars, etc).

4. CREATE /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/api/dashboard/stats/route.ts — A new endpoint that returns real-time dashboard KPIs from Supabase: total appointments today, total revenue this week, total clients, average rating, upcoming appointments count. The main dashboard page should call this instead of computing stats client-side.

5. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx — Wire the KPI cards at the top to call the new GET /api/dashboard/stats endpoint. Replace any hardcoded or mock metrics with real data. Keep the Supabase real-time subscription for live appointment updates.

IMPORTANT: When creating Supabase clients in API routes, do NOT initialize them at the top-level module scope. Use a lazy getter function like: function getSupabase() { return createClient(url, key); } — this prevents build failures when env vars are not available at build time.

After all changes, run: npm run build
The build MUST pass with zero TypeScript errors.

Phase 4: Wire Barber CRM Dashboard to Real Data + Fix Critical Bugs

The Barber CRM at /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/ has a working booking flow but the dashboard is mostly mock data. Fix these issues:

1. CREATE /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/appointments/page.tsx — The sidebar links to this route but the page doesn't exist (404). Build a full appointments management page with:
   - Table showing all appointments from GET /api/appointments
   - Status filter tabs (all, confirmed, pending, completed, cancelled, no-show)
   - Ability to update appointment status via PATCH
   - Search by client name or barber
   - Use the existing dark theme with gold accents (match other dashboard pages)

2. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/staff/page.tsx — Revenue stats use Math.random() (line ~76). Wire to the real GET /api/staff endpoint which calls getBarberStats() from supabase.ts. Remove all mock random data.

3. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx — The Add Client dialog does console.log instead of calling POST /api/clients. Wire the form submission to the API. Show success/error toast instead of just closing the dialog.

4. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/portal/page.tsx line ~323 — reviewedAppointmentIds is built from data.reviews.map(r => r.id) but should be data.reviews.map(r => r.appointment_id). This causes the Reviewed badge to never show.

5. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/api/payments/webhook/route.ts — The TODO at line ~47 says to update appointment payment status. After checkout.session.completed, update the appointment payment_status to paid in Supabase using the appointment_id from session metadata.

6. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx — Save Changes and Add Service buttons just console.log. Wire to Supabase: save business info to a settings table or locations table, save services to the services table.

7. CREATE migration 003_notification_history.sql in /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/supabase/migrations/ for the notification_history table (referenced in /api/notifications but never created).

After making changes, run: cd /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app && npm run build
Verify the build succeeds with no TypeScript errors.

Fix the KDS Supabase query in /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/page.tsx to prevent loading all-time order history.

Current problem: The fetchOrders function queries ALL orders with status in ['received', 'preparing', 'ready', 'completed'] with NO date filter and NO limit. On a busy restaurant this loads thousands of historical orders.

Fix the Supabase query (around line 314-320) to:
1. Add .gte('created_at', new Date(new Date().setHours(0,0,0,0)).toISOString()) to only fetch today's orders
2. Add .limit(50) to cap results  
3. Filter out 'completed' status from the active orders query — completed orders should not show in the KDS swimlane. Only fetch ['received', 'preparing', 'ready']
4. Apply the existing ACTIVE_ORDERS_LIMIT and COMPLETED_ORDERS_PER_PAGE constants that are defined but unused

The file is at /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/page.tsx

Fix the double order insertion bug in Delhi Palace checkout. 

The problem: /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/checkout/page.tsx calls saveOrderToSupabase() which inserts an order BEFORE Stripe payment. Then the Stripe webhook at /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/api/webhook/route.ts ALSO inserts an order on checkout.session.completed. This creates duplicate orders in the KDS.

Fix: Remove the saveOrderToSupabase() call from checkout/page.tsx for Stripe payments. Only insert orders via the webhook after payment succeeds. For in-store/cash payments (no Stripe), keep the direct insert. 

Also move @supabase/supabase-js from devDependencies to dependencies in /root/Delhi-Palace/package.json.

Also create /root/Delhi-Palace/public/sounds/ directory and add a note that beep.mp3 is needed, or update /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/beep.ts to only use Web Audio API and remove the MP3 fetch attempt.

The receive_sms should be a webhook endpoint at /webhook/twilio that receives incoming SMS and forwards them to the PA's Telegram for notification. Add both tools to the MCP tool registry so agents can use them. Include proper error handling and rate limiting (max 10 SMS per hour).

jobs.task: SDK Integration Test: Read /root/openclaw/gateway.py and count how many API endpoints are defined (routes with @app.get, @app.post, etc). Report the exact count.

Add a "Today's Agenda" section to the main dashboard page at /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx.

REQUIREMENTS:
1. Add a new section BELOW the existing KPI cards showing today's appointments as a vertical timeline
2. Each timeline entry shows: time (e.g. '2:00 PM'), client name, service name, barber name, and a status Badge
3. Add a 'Check In' Button next to each pending/confirmed appointment that calls POST /api/appointments/checkin
4. Create a new API route at src/app/api/appointments/checkin/route.ts that accepts JSON {appointmentId: string} and updates the appointment status to 'checked_in' in Supabase
5. After check-in, the badge should change to green 'Checked In' without page reload (optimistic UI update)
6. Include a 'No appointments today' empty state when list is empty
7. Add mock data fallback with 5 sample appointments for today if the API fetch fails

STYLING RULES:
- Dark theme: bg-dark-900, border-dark-800, text-white, text-dark-400 for secondary text
- Gold accents: text-gold-500, bg-gold-600 for primary buttons
- Font classes: font-heading for titles, font-body for body text  
- Use animate-in fade-in for entry animations

COMPONENT RULES - CRITICAL:
- ONLY import from these existing UI components: Button (@/components/ui/button), Badge (@/components/ui/badge), Input (@/components/ui/input)
- ONLY import icons from lucide-react (Clock, User, CheckCircle, Calendar, etc.)
- Do NOT import Card, ScrollArea, Avatar, Tabs, or any component not listed above
- Do NOT install new npm packages
- Use standard HTML divs with Tailwind classes for layout

The dashboard page is a client component ("use client") that fetches from /api/dashboard/stats and /api/appointments.
Read the existing page.tsx first before modifying it to preserve existing content.

Create a simple FAQ page at src/app/dashboard/faq/page.tsx with 5 hardcoded barbershop FAQs displayed as expandable accordion items. Use a client component with useState for open/close state. Style with dark-900 backgrounds, gold-500 accents, font-heading class. Only import Button from @/components/ui/button and ChevronDown from lucide-react. No other imports needed.

Add a client portal page at /dashboard/portal that shows a simple welcome message with the text "Welcome to your Client Portal" styled in the dark/gold theme matching other dashboard pages. Use only existing UI components (Button, Input, Badge from @/components/ui/). The page should have a heading and a paragraph about viewing appointment history. This is a single-file task — create src/app/dashboard/portal/page.tsx only.

Phase 6: Build 10 features. All work in /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/.

TASK 1: Create /src/app/api/loyalty/route.ts - GET returns client loyalty points, POST adds points. Use lazy Supabase pattern.
TASK 2: Create /src/app/dashboard/loyalty/page.tsx - Leaderboard table with tiers (Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum).
TASK 3: Create /src/app/api/reports/route.ts - GET with type param (daily/weekly/monthly), support CSV export.
TASK 4: Create /src/app/dashboard/reports/page.tsx - Report cards with date range tabs and CSV export button.
TASK 5: Create /src/app/api/messages/route.ts - GET message threads, POST send message. Lazy Supabase.
TASK 6: Create /src/app/dashboard/messages/page.tsx - Chat UI with conversation list and message thread.
TASK 7: Create /src/app/api/appointments/reschedule/route.ts - POST reschedule with conflict check.
TASK 8: Update /src/app/dashboard/appointments/page.tsx - Add reschedule button with modal.
TASK 9: Update /src/app/dashboard/layout.tsx - Add Loyalty, Reports, Messages nav links.
TASK 10: Run cd /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app && rm -rf .next && npm run build. Fix errors. Then git add -A && git commit -m "Phase 6: Loyalty, Reports, Messages, Rescheduling" && git push origin main

Phase 4b: Build real-time analytics dashboard + SMS appointment reminders for Barber CRM

The Barber CRM at /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/ needs its analytics and notification systems completed.

1. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/revenue/page.tsx — The monthly revenue trend chart uses hardcoded static data instead of real Supabase queries. Wire it to GET /api/revenue which already exists. The line chart should show actual monthly revenue from completed appointments. The pie chart should show real revenue breakdown by service type.

2. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/notifications/page.tsx — The notification history shows 3 hardcoded mock entries. Wire it to GET /api/notifications which has Supabase integration. Show real notification history with status (sent/failed), timestamps, and recipient info. Add a button to manually send an SMS reminder for upcoming appointments (call POST /api/notifications).

3. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/reviews/page.tsx — Check if reviews page fetches from GET /api/reviews or uses mock data. If mock, wire to real Supabase data. Add average rating display and rating distribution bar chart (5 stars, 4 stars, etc).

4. CREATE /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/api/dashboard/stats/route.ts — A new endpoint that returns real-time dashboard KPIs from Supabase: total appointments today, total revenue this week, total clients, average rating, upcoming appointments count. The main dashboard page should call this instead of computing stats client-side.

5. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx — Wire the KPI cards at the top to call the new GET /api/dashboard/stats endpoint. Replace any hardcoded or mock metrics with real data. Keep the Supabase real-time subscription for live appointment updates.

IMPORTANT: When creating Supabase clients in API routes, do NOT initialize them at the top-level module scope. Use a lazy getter function like: function getSupabase() { return createClient(url, key); } — this prevents build failures when env vars are not available at build time.

After all changes, run: npm run build
The build MUST pass with zero TypeScript errors.

jobs.task:
Phase 4: Wire Barber CRM Dashboard to Real Data + Fix Critical Bugs

The Barber CRM at /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/ has a working booking flow but the dashboard is mostly mock data. Fix these issues:

1. CREATE /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/appointments/page.tsx — The sidebar links to this route but the page doesn't exist (404). Build a full appointments management page with:
   - Table showing all appointments from GET /api/appointments
   - Status filter tabs (all, confirmed, pending, completed, cancelled, no-show)
   - Ability to update appointment status via PATCH
   - Search by client name or barber
   - Use the existing dark theme with gold accents (match other dashboard pages)

2. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/staff/page.tsx — Revenue stats use Math.random() (line ~76). Wire to the real GET /api/staff endpoint which calls getBarberStats() from supabase.ts. Remove all mock random data.

3. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/clients/page.tsx — The Add Client dialog does console.log instead of calling POST /api/clients. Wire the form submission to the API. Show success/error toast instead of just closing the dialog.

4. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/portal/page.tsx line ~323 — reviewedAppointmentIds is built from data.reviews.map(r => r.id) but should be data.reviews.map(r => r.appointment_id). This causes the Reviewed badge to never show.

5. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/api/payments/webhook/route.ts — The TODO at line ~47 says to update appointment payment status. After checkout.session.completed, update the appointment payment_status to paid in Supabase using the appointment_id from session metadata.

6. FIX /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/src/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx — Save Changes and Add Service buttons just console.log. Wire to Supabase: save business info to a settings table or locations table, save services to the services table.

7. CREATE migration 003_notification_history.sql in /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app/supabase/migrations/ for the notification_history table (referenced in /api/notifications but never created).

After making changes, run: cd /root/Barber-CRM/nextjs-app && npm run build
Verify the build succeeds with no TypeScript errors.

Fix the KDS Supabase query in /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/page.tsx to prevent loading all-time order history.

Current problem: The fetchOrders function queries ALL orders with status in ['received', 'preparing', 'ready', 'completed'] with NO date filter and NO limit. On a busy restaurant this loads thousands of historical orders.

Fix the Supabase query (around line 314-320) to:
1. Add .gte('created_at', new Date(new Date().setHours(0,0,0,0)).toISOString()) to only fetch today's orders
2. Add .limit(50) to cap results  
3. Filter out 'completed' status from the active orders query — completed orders should not show in the KDS swimlane. Only fetch ['received', 'preparing', 'ready']
4. Apply the existing ACTIVE_ORDERS_LIMIT and COMPLETED_ORDERS_PER_PAGE constants that are defined but unused

The file is at /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/page.tsx

Fix the double order insertion bug in Delhi Palace checkout. 

The problem: /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/checkout/page.tsx calls saveOrderToSupabase() which inserts an order BEFORE Stripe payment. Then the Stripe webhook at /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/api/webhook/route.ts ALSO inserts an order on checkout.session.completed. This creates duplicate orders in the KDS.

Fix: Remove the saveOrderToSupabase() call from checkout/page.tsx for Stripe payments. Only insert orders via the webhook after payment succeeds. For in-store/cash payments (no Stripe), keep the direct insert. 

Also move @supabase/supabase-js from devDependencies to dependencies in /root/Delhi-Palace/package.json.

Also create /root/Delhi-Palace/public/sounds/ directory and add a note that beep.mp3 is needed, or update /root/Delhi-Palace/src/app/kds/beep.ts to only use Web Audio API and remove the MP3 fetch attempt.
